summary: Sprint 18 — CREW BATTLE ENGINE full test: backend (21/21 pytest) + frontend ARENA tab (21/21 UI checks). All features verified: LiveBattleDashboard (SHADOW SQUAD vs NEXUS ELITE + IRON WOLVES), MatchmakingPanel (IL TUO KORE 82.4, MATCH BUONO labels, IN CORSO state), CONTRIBUISCI ORA navigation to /nexus-trigger, ELITE ACTIVITY FEED SFIDA buttons open ChallengeInviteModal with DNA bars. One backend intermittent bug found (timezone-naive/aware datetime comparison).

minor.issue: Intermittent 500 error: 'TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes'. All three endpoints use datetime.utcnow() (naive) but battles seeded with datetime.now(timezone.utc) (aware) cause comparison failure. Fix: replace all datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc) in these endpoints.

Pre-existing 500 error: 'TypeError: can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes'. Same root cause — datetime.utcnow() vs timezone-aware stored value. Seen in backend logs during NEXUS tab load. Not related to this sprint but same fix applies.

integration_issues.flow: MatchmakingPanel SFIDA button → SFIDA LANCIATA alert
integration_issues.issue: UI test for SFIDA button (loading + alert) could not be performed: both NEXUS ELITE and IRON WOLVES are already in active battles (IRON WOLVES was challenged during API backend testing). Matchmakingpanel correctly shows 'IN CORSO' for both. API-level test confirmed: challenge endpoint returns {status: battle_started} on first call and 400 on duplicate — working correctly.

react_native_issues.issue: STEFANO's DNA is missing 'agilita' and 'potenza' keys (seeded without those fields). Shows 0 for AGI and POT in the DNA comparison. Not a code bug — data gap in seed. Only cosmetic.
Component and data array defined in arena.tsx (lines 36-254) but NEVER used in ArenaTab render — dead code. Has no functional impact but increases bundle size.

action_items: MEDIUM: Fix datetime timezone mismatch in get_live_crew_battles (line 1249), challenge_crew_to_battle (line 1405), and contribute_to_crew_battle (line 1457) — replace datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc) to prevent intermittent 500 errors when battle ends_at is timezone-aware
LOW: Remove dead code: EliteDivisionUpdates component + DIVISION_UPDATES array in arena.tsx (never rendered)

INFO: SFIDA button UI flow in MatchmakingPanel not UI-tested — IRON WOLVES was challenged during API backend testing. API-level verification confirms it works. A fresh test session without prior API challenges would be needed to UI-test the SFIDA button flow.
critical_code_review_comments: get_live_crew_battles line 1249: now = datetime.utcnow() — should be datetime.now(timezone.utc) to match MongoDB-stored timezone-aware datetimes. This caused intermittent 500 errors visible in backend logs.
challenge_crew_to_battle line 1405 and contribute_to_crew_battle line 1457 also use datetime.utcnow() — same potential issue.
EliteDivisionUpdates component (lines 211-253) and DIVISION_UPDATES array (lines 36-43) are dead code — defined but never rendered in ArenaTab.

success_rate.frontend: 95% — All visual/functional checks pass. SFIDA button flow in MatchmakingPanel not UI-tested due to both available crews already being in active battles from API testing.

seed_data_creation: Created 1 additional crew battle (SHADOW SQUAD vs IRON WOLVES) during API testing of challenge endpoint. This is now visible in BATTLE LIVE section as a second live battle.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Arena tab with Crew Battle Engine is fully functional. Backend has 21/21 tests passing. One medium bug: datetime.utcnow() vs datetime.now(timezone.utc) inconsistency in crew battle endpoints — intermittent 500 risk. Two live battles now active: SHADOW SQUAD vs NEXUS ELITE (ends ~16:31 UTC today) and SHADOW SQUAD vs IRON WOLVES (ends ~15:35 UTC tomorrow). Both crews in matchmake show 'IN CORSO'. To test SFIDA button flow fresh, battles must expire or be removed. ELITE ACTIVITY FEED and ChallengeInviteModal working correctly.

rca_of_issue.root_cause: Intermittent 500 on GET /battles/crew/live: get_live_crew_battles() uses datetime.utcnow() (timezone-naive) at line 1249 to compare against battle ends_at. If ends_at was stored as timezone-aware datetime (e.g., datetime.now(timezone.utc)), Python raises TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Create a crew battle with datetime.now(timezone.utc) for ends_at. 2. Call GET /battles/crew/live. 3. Server throws 500.
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Replace all datetime.utcnow() calls in crew battle endpoints (lines 1249, 1405, 1457) with datetime.now(timezone.utc). The challenge endpoint at line 1405 already uses datetime.utcnow() for the new battles it creates — these are naive and work fine. The issue is with battles seeded using timezone-aware datetimes.
